finding: A lane adoption event was written under `.atm/history/session-events/...` as an untracked file and pre-commit classified it as manual-review residue.
expectedBehavior: Ephemeral lane/session events should either be written under ignored runtime storage or be lifecycle-managed into a compact tracked digest; they should not leave ambiguous untracked history files that block or distract later governed work.
evidenceOrRepro: After ATM-GOV-0195 closeback, `git status --short` showed `?? .atm/history/session-events/lane-20260719134847-codex-governance-optimizer-ef8a433602/`; pre-commit residueReport classified the adopt JSON as `manual-review` with reason `path is not recognized as a known ATM auto-generated residue shape`.
followUp: Decide whether lane session events are runtime logs or tracked evidence. If runtime, move them to `.atm/runtime/session-events` and gitignore them; if evidence, add deterministic seal/cleanup and pre-commit recognition.
